more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: Zimbabwe’s Shocking Social Media Ban: Are They Protecting Children or Silencing a Generation’s Voice Online?
more.description: HARARE — The Zimbabwean government is currently drafting a controversial new policy that could see children under the age of 18 banned from using social media platforms. Framed as a cornerstone of a new ‘Child Online Protection Policy’, the move has ignited a fierce national debate over the balance between safeguarding minors and the state’s [...]The post Zimbabwe’s Shocking Social Media Ban: Are They Protecting Children or Silencing a Generation’s Voice Online? first appeared on My Zimbabwe News.

more.title: Zimbabwe lacks capacity to detect 17 rare earth minerals in lithium exports at its borders
more.description: Zimbabwe currently does not have the technology at its borders to detect the full range of rare earth minerals that may be contained within lithium or other mineral exports, the Minister of Mines and Mining Development, Polite Kambamura, has told Parliament.The post Zimbabwe lacks capacity to detect 17 rare earth minerals in lithium exports at its borders appeared first on Nehanda Radio.

more.title: Muzarabani earns spot in ICC T20 World Cup Team of the Tournament
more.description: PACE bowler Blessing Muzarabani has been named in the recently ended T20 Team of the Tournament. The announcement was made by the International Cricket Council (ICC), following India’s successful title defence against New Zealand, naming Muzarabani in the team of the tournament alongside global icons like Jasprit Bumrah and Sanju Samson. He is the only [...]The post Muzarabani earns spot in ICC T20 World Cup Team of the Tournament appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com.

more.title: 11 years on, rights groups demand answers over Itai Dzamara disappearance
more.description: HUMAN rights organisations have renewed calls for justice over the disappearance of pro-democracy activist Itai Dzamara, who was abducted 11 years ago. Dzamara disappeared on March 9, 2015, after he was allegedly taken by unidentified men at a barber shop in Glen View, Harare, in broad daylight. He has not been seen since. Before his [...]The post 11 years on, rights groups demand answers over Itai Dzamara disappearance appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com.

more.title: Sheriff cleared to seize Mahere’s property to recover US$33k in Machakaire lawsuit
more.description: The High Court of Zimbabwe has ordered the attachment of property belonging to lawyer and former Mt Pleasant legislator Fadzayi Mahere valued at about US$33 395 following a defamation lawsuit filed by the Minister of Youth, Empowerment, Development and Vocational Training, Tinoda Machakaire.The post Sheriff cleared to seize Mahere’s property to recover US$33k in Machakaire lawsuit appeared first on Nehanda Radio.
